recurring.name: Muharram (Islamic New Year)
recurring.description: First month of the Islamic calendar. The month of Allah.

recurring.name: Ashura
recurring.description: Day of fasting — Moses was saved from Pharaoh. Day of mourning for Shia Muslims (martyrdom of Husain RA).

recurring.name: Mawlid an-Nabawi
recurring.description: Birth of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ (12 Rabi al-Awwal).

recurring.name: Isra wal Mi'raj
recurring.description: Night Journey and Ascension of the Prophet ﷺ to the heavens.

recurring.name: Sha'ban 15 (Shab-e-Barat)
recurring.description: Night of Forgiveness — night of worship and seeking forgiveness.

recurring.name: Start of Ramadan
recurring.description: Beginning of the holy month of fasting.

recurring.name: Laylat ul-Qadr
recurring.description: Night of Power — better than a thousand months.

recurring.name: Eid ul-Fitr
recurring.description: Festival of breaking the fast — celebration after Ramadan.

recurring.name: Arafah Day
recurring.description: Day of Hajj — standing at Arafah. Fasting on this day expiates two years of sins.

recurring.name: Eid ul-Adha
recurring.description: Festival of Sacrifice — commemorating Ibrahim's AS willingness to sacrifice his son.

recurring.name: Days of Tashreeq
recurring.description: Days of Eid (11th, 12th, 13th Dhul Hijjah) — Qurbani can be done on these days.
